title: Learning Latent Dynamics for Planning from Pixels
key_contribution: Planning in learned latent dynamics from pixels
main_limitation: Limited visual realism and domain breadth by current standards
relevance_to_survey: Canonical bridge from video prediction to world models

title: Dream to Control: Learning Behaviors by Latent Imagination
key_contribution: Latent imagination with actor-critic learning
main_limitation: Still tied to task rewards and online interaction
relevance_to_survey: Essential for planning/control-oriented world-model taxonomy

title: Recurrent World Models Facilitate Policy Evolution
key_contribution: Popularized the modern world-model pipeline
main_limitation: Small-scale by later standards
relevance_to_survey: Foundational framing paper for the whole survey

title: Action-Conditional Video Prediction using Deep Networks in Atari Games
authors: Junhyuk Oh; Xiaoxiao Guo; Honglak Lee; Richard L. Lewis; Satinder Singh
key_contribution: Early long-horizon action-conditioned prediction from pixels
main_limitation: Game-domain scope and pixel-space brittleness
relevance_to_survey: Key origin point for predictive models from visual interaction

title: Unsupervised Learning for Physical Interaction through Video Prediction
authors: Chelsea Finn; Ian Goodfellow; Sergey Levine
key_contribution: Robot interaction learned through video prediction
main_limitation: Short-horizon and pixel-space limitations
relevance_to_survey: Core robot-world-model precursor

title: Stochastic Variational Video Prediction
key_contribution: Introduced latent-variable stochastic futures to video prediction
main_limitation: Still optimized largely around reconstruction
relevance_to_survey: Important for uncertainty in predictive world models

title: Interaction Networks for Learning about Objects, Relations and Physics
key_contribution: General-purpose learnable interaction simulator
main_limitation: Not video-based in its core formulation
relevance_to_survey: Foundational background for structured simulators and object interactions

title: Visual Interaction Networks
key_contribution: Combines visual perception with interaction-based physical dynamics
main_limitation: Synthetic settings dominate
relevance_to_survey: One of the clearest visual-physics sequence models

title: Reasoning About Physical Interactions with Object-Oriented Prediction and Planning
key_contribution: Maps images to objects, predicts object interactions, renders back to pixels
main_limitation: Focused on structured synthetic object worlds
relevance_to_survey: Central object-centric world-model paper for intuitive physics

title: DINO-WM: World Models on Pre-trained Visual Features enable Zero-shot Planning
authors: Gaoyue Zhou; Hengkai Pan; Yann LeCun; Lerrel Pinto
key_contribution: Predicts DINOv2 features for zero-shot planning
main_limitation: Very recent and still maturing
relevance_to_survey: Important recent alternative to pixel reconstruction

title: Video Diffusion Models
key_contribution: Established diffusion as a serious video-model baseline
main_limitation: Not a world model in the control sense by itself
relevance_to_survey: Essential background for later diffusion world models

title: Diffusion for World Modeling: Visual Details Matter in Atari
key_contribution: Makes diffusion explicit as a world-model architecture
main_limitation: Focused on game environments
relevance_to_survey: Directly relevant modern diffusion-based world model

title: Genie: Generative Interactive Environments
key_contribution: Learns playable interactive environments from unlabeled video
main_limitation: Early foundation-world-model stage with many open questions
relevance_to_survey: High-value recent reference for interactive generative environments

title: Transformers are Sample-Efficient World Models
authors: Vincent Micheli; Eloi Alonso; François Fleuret
key_contribution: Recasts world modeling as token prediction with a transformer
main_limitation: Domain scope is still mainly Atari
relevance_to_survey: Important transformer-based world-model milestone

title: Mastering Atari with Discrete World Models
key_contribution: Shows strong Atari performance with discrete latent world models
main_limitation: Still benchmark-centric
relevance_to_survey: Key successor to Dreamer in discrete latent space

title: DayDreamer: World Models for Physical Robot Learning
key_contribution: Demonstrates world-model learning directly on physical robots
main_limitation: Requires substantial platform engineering
relevance_to_survey: Important embodied world-model paper

title: Contrastive Learning of Structured World Models
key_contribution: Uses contrastive learning to induce structured object-centric world models
main_limitation: Often evaluated in simplified environments
relevance_to_survey: Very useful for structured latent-dynamics taxonomy

title: SlotFormer: Unsupervised Visual Dynamics Simulation with Object-Centric Models
key_contribution: Simulates dynamics in slot space for object-centric prediction
main_limitation: Still depends on object-centric pre-processing quality
relevance_to_survey: Strong modern object-centric dynamics reference

title: Entity Abstraction in Visual Model-Based Reinforcement Learning
authors: Rishi Veerapaneni; John D. Co-Reyes; Michael Chang; Michael Janner; Chelsea Finn; Jiajun Wu; Joshua B. Tenenbaum; Sergey Levine
key_contribution: OP3 is a key object-centric MBRL paper connecting unsupervised perception, dynamics, and planning
main_limitation: UNKNOWN
relevance_to_survey: Restored from ranked core paper list

title: MCVD: Masked Conditional Video Diffusion for Prediction, Generation, and Interpolation
authors: Vikram Voleti; Alexia Jolicoeur-Martineau; Christopher Pal
key_contribution: Direct diffusion formulation for video prediction and interpolation
main_limitation: Not yet a full planning/control world model
relevance_to_survey: Important diffusion-prediction bridge paper

title: VideoWorld: Exploring Knowledge Learning from Unlabeled Videos
key_contribution: Claims knowledge and control can emerge from unlabeled videos via latent dynamics
main_limitation: Very recent and broad in scope
relevance_to_survey: Potentially important for video-only world-model scaling

title: Vid2World: Crafting Video Diffusion Models to Interactive World Models
key_contribution: Transforms passive video diffusion models into interactive world models
main_limitation: Very recent and not yet broadly adopted
relevance_to_survey: Highly relevant newest paper at the diffusion/world-model boundary

title: PhyDNet: Disentangling Physical Dynamics From Unknown Factors for Unsupervised Video Prediction
key_contribution: Separates physical dynamics from residual appearance factors
main_limitation: Physics prior is still approximate and narrow
relevance_to_survey: Directly relevant to physical-dynamics learning from video

title: MaskViT: Masked Visual Pre-Training for Video Prediction
key_contribution: Masked-token video prediction scaled to planning use
main_limitation: Still primarily a predictive model rather than a full world model
relevance_to_survey: Useful bridge between video prediction and embodied planning

title: Eidetic 3D LSTM: A Model for Video Prediction and Beyond
key_contribution: Strong 3D recurrent architecture for spatiotemporal prediction
main_limitation: Still largely fidelity-oriented
relevance_to_survey: Good representative of mature recurrent video prediction

title: SimVP: Simpler yet Better Video Prediction
key_contribution: Strong simple baseline for spatiotemporal prediction
main_limitation: Not inherently structured or interactive
relevance_to_survey: Useful baseline anchor in any survey section on later video prediction

title: Decomposing Motion and Content for Natural Video Sequence Prediction
key_contribution: Separates motion from content for prediction
main_limitation: Still limited by reconstruction quality
relevance_to_survey: Important historical factorization idea

title: Stochastic Video Generation with a Learned Prior
key_contribution: Learns a prior over stochastic future trajectories
main_limitation: Limited control/planning framing
relevance_to_survey: Important stochastic latent-video reference

title: Learning Visual Predictive Models of Physics for Playing Billiards
key_contribution: Early object-based physical prediction from visuals
main_limitation: Highly constrained domain
relevance_to_survey: Useful historical background for visual physics

title: A Compositional Object-Based Approach to Learning Physical Dynamics
key_contribution: Classic neural physics engine style compositional dynamics
main_limitation: Not video-first
relevance_to_survey: Important conceptual precursor to object-centric world models

title: Unsupervised Learning of Object Structure and Dynamics from Videos
key_contribution: Learns keypoint structure and dynamics without supervision
main_limitation: Keypoint abstraction can be coarse
relevance_to_survey: Useful for object-centric representation learning from video

title: Pre-training Contextualized World Models with In-the-wild Videos for Reinforcement Learning
key_contribution: Pretrains world models on in-the-wild videos for transfer
main_limitation: Representation and transfer remain domain-sensitive
relevance_to_survey: Important for large-scale video pretraining discussions

title: PHYRE: A New Benchmark for Physical Reasoning
key_contribution: Benchmark for physical reasoning and generalization
main_limitation: 2D puzzle world abstraction
relevance_to_survey: Foundational benchmark background for physical world modeling

title: CLEVRER: CoLlision Events for Video REpresentation and Reasoning
key_contribution: Targets descriptive, explanatory, predictive, and counterfactual reasoning in videos
main_limitation: Synthetic and QA-style
relevance_to_survey: Important for causal/physical reasoning evaluation

title: Physion: Evaluating Physical Prediction from Vision in Humans and Machines
key_contribution: Large-scale benchmark for visual physical prediction
main_limitation: Simulation to perception gap remains
relevance_to_survey: One of the most important physical-prediction benchmarks

title: Towards Accurate Generative Models of Video: A New Metric & Challenges
key_contribution: Introduced Fréchet Video Distance
main_limitation: Metric does not test controllability or physics directly
relevance_to_survey: Essential evaluation background for generative video sections

title: VideoPhy: Evaluating Physical Commonsense for Video Generation
key_contribution: Evaluates whether generated videos obey physical commonsense
main_limitation: Focused on commonsense prompts rather than full interaction
relevance_to_survey: Important modern physics benchmark for video generation

title: Towards World Simulator: Crafting Physical Commonsense-Based Benchmark for Video Generation
key_contribution: Builds a physics-commonsense benchmark and evaluation framework
main_limitation: Still benchmarking prompt-based generation rather than embodied control
relevance_to_survey: Useful for recent evaluation chapter and open-challenges discussion

title: How Far Is Video Generation from World Model: A Physical Law Perspective
key_contribution: Directly tests whether video models generalize physical laws
main_limitation: Uses simplified synthetic mechanics testbed
relevance_to_survey: Extremely relevant to the survey thesis itself

title: Do generative video models learn physical principles from watching videos?
key_contribution: Introduces Physics-IQ to disentangle realism from physical understanding
main_limitation: Very recent and still expanding
relevance_to_survey: High-value current reference on realism vs physics understanding

title: Convolutional LSTM Network: A Machine Learning Approach for Precipitation Nowcasting
authors: Xingjian Shi; Zhourong Chen; Hao Wang; Dit-Yan Yeung; Wai-kin Wong; Wang-chun Woo
key_contribution: Introduces ConvLSTM as a canonical spatiotemporal recurrent baseline
main_limitation: Not a world model and lacks action conditioning or explicit physical abstraction
relevance_to_survey: Baseline anchor for Section 04 discussion of recurrent visual dynamics

title: PredRNN: A Recurrent Neural Network for Spatiotemporal Predictive Learning
authors: Yunbo Wang; Haixu Wu; Jianjin Zhang; Zhifeng Gao; Jianmin Wang; Philip S. Yu; Mingsheng Long
key_contribution: Family anchor for PredRNN-style deep-in-time recurrent predictive learning
main_limitation: Retrieved source is a later arXiv family anchor rather than the original 2017 conference version
relevance_to_survey: Supports Section 04 baseline lineage beyond SV2P and robot video prediction

title: PredRNN++: Towards A Resolution of the Deep-in-Time Dilemma in Spatiotemporal Predictive Learning
authors: Yunbo Wang; Zhifeng Gao; Mingsheng Long; Jianmin Wang; Philip S. Yu
key_contribution: Addresses deep-in-time recurrent prediction with causal LSTM and gradient highway mechanisms
main_limitation: Primarily architecture-oriented and not an explicit world model
relevance_to_survey: Comparator for Section 04 architecture-oriented video prediction baselines

title: Memory In Memory: A Predictive Neural Network for Learning Higher-Order Non-Stationarity from Spatiotemporal Dynamics
authors: Yunbo Wang; Jianjin Zhang; Hongyu Zhu; Mingsheng Long; Jianmin Wang; Philip S. Yu
key_contribution: Models higher-order non-stationarity in recurrent spatiotemporal prediction
main_limitation: Architecture-oriented baseline without explicit world-model semantics
relevance_to_survey: Strengthens Section 04 coverage of recurrent video prediction baselines

title: VideoGPT: Video Generation using VQ-VAE and Transformers
authors: Wilson Yan; Yunzhi Zhang; Pieter Abbeel; Aravind Srinivas
key_contribution: Uses VQ-VAE and Transformers for tokenized video generation
main_limitation: Not a world model unless connected to prediction or control
relevance_to_survey: Supports distinctions among video generation token modeling and predictive world models

title: Object-Centric Learning with Slot Attention
authors: Francesco Locatello; Dirk Weissenborn; Thomas Unterthiner; Aravindh Mahendran; Georg Heigold; Jakob Uszkoreit; Alexey Dosovitskiy; Thomas Kipf
key_contribution: Introduces Slot Attention as a foundational object-centric representation mechanism
main_limitation: Representation paper rather than a dynamics or control world model
relevance_to_survey: Foundation for slot-based world-model discussion in Section 05

title: Conditional Object-Centric Learning from Video
authors: Thomas Kipf; Gamaleldin F. Elsayed; Aravindh Mahendran; Austin Stone; Sara Sabour; Georg Heigold; Rico Jonschkowski; Alexey Dosovitskiy; Klaus Greff
key_contribution: Extends object-centric learning to video with temporal conditioning
main_limitation: Mostly representation-focused rather than a complete predictive simulator
relevance_to_survey: Supports temporal consistency and video object-centric learning in Section 05

title: SAVi++: Towards End-to-End Object-Centric Learning from Real-World Videos
authors: Gamaleldin F. Elsayed; Aravindh Mahendran; Sjoerd van Steenkiste; Klaus Greff; Michael C. Mozer; Thomas Kipf
key_contribution: Moves object-centric video learning toward real-world videos
main_limitation: Still representation-centered and not a full physical dynamics model
relevance_to_survey: Supports Section 05 discussion beyond synthetic object-centric settings

title: Object-Centric Learning for Real-World Videos by Predicting Temporal Feature Similarities
authors: Andrii Zadaianchuk; Maximilian Seitzer; Georg Martius
key_contribution: Uses temporal feature similarity prediction for object-centric real-world video learning
main_limitation: Candidate paper requiring careful note extraction before strong claims
relevance_to_survey: Extends Section 05 real-world object-centric video coverage

title: Mastering Diverse Domains through World Models
authors: Danijar Hafner; Jurgis Pasukonis; Jimmy Ba; Timothy Lillicrap
key_contribution: Extends Dreamer-style world models across diverse domains
main_limitation: Still reward-centric and not a physical-law evaluation by itself
relevance_to_survey: Keeps Section 06 latent world model lineage current beyond Dreamer 2020

title: iVideoGPT: Interactive VideoGPTs are Scalable World Models
authors: Jialong Wu; Shaofeng Yin; Ningya Feng; Xu He; Dong Li; Jianye Hao; Mingsheng Long
key_contribution: Connects tokenized video generation to interactive world modeling
main_limitation: Candidate paper requiring detailed notes before manuscript claims
relevance_to_survey: Bridge paper for Sections 06 and 07

title: Video generation models as world simulators
authors: Tim Brooks; Bill Peebles; Connor Holmes; Will DePue; Yufei Guo
key_contribution: Frames scaled video generation as a possible route toward world simulators
main_limitation: Technical report claims should be treated cautiously and not as proof of physical validity
relevance_to_survey: Section 07 should discuss this as a contested simulator framing

title: Learning Interactive Real-World Simulators
authors: Sherry Yang; Yilun Du; Kamyar Ghasemipour; Jonathan Tompson; Leslie Kaelbling; Dale Schuurmans; Pieter Abbeel
key_contribution: Targets interactive real-world simulation rather than passive video realism
main_limitation: Requires detailed note extraction before comparing mechanisms
relevance_to_survey: Core Section 07 evidence for interactivity as a distinct goal

title: GAIA-1: A Generative World Model for Autonomous Driving
authors: Anthony Hu; Lloyd Russell; Hudson Yeo; Zak Murez; George Fedoseev; Alex Kendall; Jamie Shotton; Gianluca Corrado
key_contribution: Applies generative world modeling to autonomous driving video
main_limitation: Domain-specific and should not be generalized to all physical simulation
relevance_to_survey: Adds driving world models to Section 07 beyond game examples

title: DriveDreamer: Towards Real-world-driven World Models for Autonomous Driving
authors: Xiaofeng Wang; Zheng Zhu; Guan Huang; Xinze Chen; Jiagang Zhu; Jiwen Lu
key_contribution: Uses real-world driving data for controllable driving video world modeling
main_limitation: Domain-specific and needs benchmark details before strong claims
relevance_to_survey: Supports Section 07 real-world-driven diffusion world models

title: DriveDreamer-2: LLM-Enhanced World Models for Diverse Driving Video Generation
authors: Guosheng Zhao; Xiaofeng Wang; Zheng Zhu; Xinze Chen; Guan Huang; Xiaoyi Bao; Xingang Wang
key_contribution: Explores language-enhanced controllable driving video generation
main_limitation: Candidate paper for semantic controllability rather than core physical validity
relevance_to_survey: Contrasts explicit semantic conditioning with latent controls in Section 07

title: Cosmos World Foundation Model Platform for Physical AI
key_contribution: Represents industrial-scale world foundation model development for physical AI
main_limitation: Platform-scale paper requiring cautious treatment and detailed notes
relevance_to_survey: Candidate Section 07 reference for world foundation model framing

title: WorldScore: A Unified Evaluation Benchmark for World Generation
authors: Haoyi Duan; Hong-Xing Yu; Sirui Chen; Li Fei-Fei; Jiajun Wu
key_contribution: Introduces a unified evaluation benchmark for world generation
main_limitation: Not specifically a physical-law benchmark and broader than video world models alone
relevance_to_survey: Candidate Section 08 addition for broader evaluation taxonomy

title: V-JEPA 2: Self-Supervised Video Models Enable Understanding, Prediction and Planning
authors: Mido Assran; Adrien Bardes; David Fan; Quentin Garrido; Russell Howes; Mojtaba Komeili; Matthew Muckley; Ammar Rizvi; Claire Roberts; Koustuv Sinha; Artem Zholus; Sergio Arnaud; Abha Gejji; Ada Martin; Francois Robert Hogan; Daniel Dugas; Piotr Bojanowski; Vasil Khalidov; Patrick Labatut; Francisco Massa; Marc Szafraniec; Kapil Krishnakumar; Yong Li; Xiaodong Ma; Sarath Chandar; Franziska Meier; Yann LeCun; Michael Rabbat; Nicolas Ballas
key_contribution: Self-supervised joint-embedding predictive video model with reported planning use through V-JEPA 2-AC
main_limitation: Requires detailed note extraction before strong claims about planning, transfer, or physical reasoning
relevance_to_survey: Candidate recent latent video world-model reference for Section 06

title: Latent Action Pretraining from Videos
authors: Seonghyeon Ye; Joel Jang; Byeongguk Jeon; Sejune Joo; Jianwei Yang; Baolin Peng; Ajay Mandlekar; Reuben Tan; Yu-Wei Chao; Bill Yuchen Lin; Lars Liden; Kimin Lee; Jianfeng Gao; Luke Zettlemoyer; Dieter Fox; Minjoon Seo
key_contribution: Learns latent actions from videos for pretraining vision-language-action models
main_limitation: Requires detailed note extraction before strong claims about transfer or comparison to action-labeled VLA models
relevance_to_survey: Candidate bridge between unlabeled video, latent actions, and embodied control

title: Diffusion Forcing: Next-token Prediction Meets Full-Sequence Diffusion
authors: Boyuan Chen; Diego Marti Monso; Yilun Du; Max Simchowitz; Russ Tedrake; Vincent Sitzmann
key_contribution: Connects next-token prediction with full-sequence diffusion through independent per-token noise levels
main_limitation: Requires detailed note extraction before strong claims about downstream planning gains or follow-up influence
relevance_to_survey: Candidate mechanism paper for Sections 04 and 07

title: WorldModelBench: Judging Video Generation Models As World Models
authors: Dacheng Li; Yunhao Fang; Yukang Chen; Shuo Yang; Shiyi Cao; Justin Wong; Michael Luo; Xiaolong Wang; Hongxu Yin; Joseph E. Gonzalez; Ion Stoica; Song Han; Yao Lu
key_contribution: Benchmark for judging video generation models as world models using instruction-following and physics-adherence criteria
main_limitation: Requires detailed note extraction before strong claims about model rankings or automatic judger reliability
relevance_to_survey: Candidate Section 08 benchmark for video world-model evaluation

title: VideoPhy-2: A Challenging Action-Centric Physical Commonsense Evaluation in Video Generation
authors: Hritik Bansal; Clark Peng; Yonatan Bitton; Roman Goldenberg; Aditya Grover; Kai-Wei Chang
key_contribution: Action-centric benchmark for evaluating physical commonsense in generated videos
main_limitation: Requires detailed note extraction before strong claims about model failure rates or automatic evaluation
relevance_to_survey: Candidate Section 08 successor to VideoPhy for physical commonsense evaluation

title: Morpheus: Benchmarking Physical Reasoning of Video Generative Models with Real Physical Experiments
authors: Chenyu Zhang; Daniil Cherniavskii; Antonios Tragoudaras; Antonios Vozikis; Thijmen Nijdam; Derck W. E. Prinzhorn; Mark Bodracska; Nicu Sebe; Andrii Zadaianchuk; Efstratios Gavves
key_contribution: Benchmarks video generative models using real physical experiments and conservation-law-guided evaluation
main_limitation: Requires detailed note extraction before strong claims about physical-law failures or evaluated model comparisons
relevance_to_survey: Candidate Section 08 reference connecting generative video evaluation to real physical experiments

title: What-If World: A Causal Benchmark for General World Models in Embodied Scenarios
authors: Kunlin Cai; Rui Song; Jinghuai Zhang; Kaiyuan Zhang; Pranav Bodapati; Alicia Yu; Fnu Suya; Mohammad Rostami; Jiaqi Ma; Yuan Tian
key_contribution: Causal benchmark using paired prompts to evaluate embodied world-model responses to changed physical details
main_limitation: Very recent preprint; requires detailed note extraction before strong claims about adoption or benchmark scope
relevance_to_survey: Candidate Section 08 reference for causal and counterfactual evaluation
